description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, giving it a ribbed appearance. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the speckled, granite-like texture of the stone. The block is solid and robust, with clean, sharp edges and a uniform height. The coloration is predominantly gray with flecks of black and lighter tones, typical of polished granite or engineered stone. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, while the top surface is visually striking due to the alternating ridges and channels. This design suggests it may serve as a tactile or drainage feature in an urban environment.
description: A rectangular, grooved stone block with a speckled granite texture and deep, parallel channels across the top surface. The block is solid, robust, and visually striking due to its ribbed pattern.
description_view_0: The block is seen from the front, showing the full width of the grooved top surface with the parallel channels running horizontally.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the channels running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front, again displaying the full width of the grooved surface with horizontal channels.
description_view_3: The opposite side view, similar to view 1, emphasizes the vertical alignment and depth of the grooves.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a tactile or drainage barrier
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained, with no signs of erosion or discoloration.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: aligned flush with pavement or adjacent blocks
